title: pelargonium × mollifolium
intro: Pelargonium × mollifolium, also known as Cape Geranium, is a hybrid species of flowering plant in the Geraniaceae family, derived from the cross between Pelargonium capitatum and Pelargonium tomentosum. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues and as an antiseptic agent due to its high content of rosmarinic acid and ursolic acid. The health benefits of Pelargonium × mollifolium include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which have been shown to alleviate symptoms of colds, sore throats, and digestive issues. The plant's medicinal properties can be extracted through various herbal preparations, such as tea, tinctures, and essential oil, which can be used internally and externally for therapeutic purposes.
